briefdescription.para: Checks whether the given namespace was already rendered or not.

simplesect.para: 1 if we already wrote this namespace or 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Processes the given node.

parameterdescription.para: the pointer to C14N context object

simplesect.para: non-negative value on success or negative value on fail

briefdescription.para: Processes all nodes in the row starting from cur.

parameterdescription.para: the pointer to C14N context object

simplesect.para: non-negative value on success or negative value on fail

briefdescription.para: Converts a string to a canonical (normalized) format.

detaileddescription.para: The code is stolen from xmlEscapeText. Added normalization of \x09, \x0a, \x0A and the mode parameter.

parameterdescription.para: the normalization mode (attribute, comment, PI or text)

simplesect.para: a normalized string (caller is responsible for calling xmlFree) or NULL if an error occurs

briefdescription.para: Handle a redefinition of memory error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlC14NCtxPtr ctxt, xmlNodePtr node, int code, const char *str1, const char *msg,...)
memberdef.name: xmlC14NErrFull

briefdescription.para: Handle a param error.
Handle an invalid node error.
Handle an unknown node error.
Handle a relative namespace error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlC14NCtxPtr ctxt, xmlNodePtr node, int error, const char *msg)
memberdef.name: xmlC14NErr

briefdescription.para: Handle an error.
Checks whether the given namespace is a default "xml:" namespace with href="http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace".

simplesect.para: 1 if the node is default or 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Compares the namespaces by names (prefixes).

simplesect.para: -1 if ns1 < ns2, 0 if ns1 == ns2 or 1 if ns1 > ns2.

briefdescription.para: Prints the given namespace to the output buffer from C14N context.

simplesect.para: 1 on success or 0 on fail.

briefdescription.para: Prints out canonical namespace axis of the current node to the buffer from C14N context as follows.

detaileddescription.para:
Namespace Axis Consider a list L containing only namespace nodes in the axis and in the node-set in lexicographic order (ascending). To begin processing L, if the first node is not the default namespace node (a node with no namespace URI and no local name), then generate a space followed by xmlns="" if and only if the following conditions are met:
the element E that owns the axis is in the node-set
The nearest ancestor element of E in the node-set has a default namespace node in the node-set (default namespace nodes always have non-empty values in XPath) The latter condition eliminates unnecessary occurrences of xmlns="" in the canonical form since an element only receives an xmlns="" if its default namespace is empty and if it has an immediate parent in the canonical form that has a non-empty default namespace. To finish processing L, simply process every namespace node in L, except omit namespace node with local name xml, which defines the xml prefix, if its string value is http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace.

Exclusive XML Canonicalization v 1.0 (http://www.w3.org/TR/xml-exc-c14n) Canonical XML applied to a document subset requires the search of the ancestor nodes of each orphan element node for attributes in the xml namespace, such as xml:lang and xml:space. These are copied into the element node except if a declaration of the same attribute is already in the attribute axis of the element (whether or not it is included in the document subset). This search and copying are omitted from the Exclusive XML Canonicalization method.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or -1 on fail.

briefdescription.para: Prints out exclusive canonical namespace axis of the current node to the buffer from C14N context as follows.

detaileddescription.para: If the element node is in the XPath subset then output the node in accordance with Canonical XML except for namespace nodes which are rendered as follows:

listitem.para:
Render each namespace node iff:
it is visibly utilized by the immediate parent element or one of its attributes, or is present in InclusiveNamespaces PrefixList, and
its prefix and value do not appear in ns_rendered. ns_rendered is obtained by popping the state stack in order to obtain a list of prefixes and their values which have already been rendered by an output ancestor of the namespace node's parent element.
Append the rendered namespace node to the list ns_rendered of namespace nodes rendered by output ancestors. Push ns_rendered on state stack and recurse.
After the recursion returns, pop thestate stack.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or -1 on fail.

briefdescription.para: Checks whether the given attribute is a default "xml:" namespace with href="http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace".

simplesect.para: 1 if the node is default or 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Prints the given attribute to the output buffer from C14N context.

parameterdescription.para: the pointer tls o first attr

simplesect.para: -1 if attr1 < attr2, 0 if attr1 == attr2 or 1 if attr1 > attr2.

briefdescription.para: Prints out canonical attribute urrent node to the buffer from C14N context as follows.

simplesect.para: 1 on success or 0 on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlC14NCtxPtr ctx, xmlNodePtr cur, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *ns)
memberdef.name: xmlC14NFindHiddenParentAttr

briefdescription.para: Finds an attribute in a hidden parent node.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the attribute node (if found) or NULL otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Fixes up the xml:base attribute.

simplesect.para: the newly created attribute or NULL

briefdescription.para: Prints out canonical attribute axis of the current node to the buffer from C14N context as follows.

detaileddescription.para: Attribute Axis In lexicographic order (ascending), process each node that is in the element's attribute axis and in the node-set.
The processing of an element node E MUST be modified slightly when an XPath node-set is given as input and the element's parent is omitted from the node-set.

Canonical XML applied to a document subset requires the search of the ancestor nodes of each orphan element node for attributes in the xml namespace, such as xml:lang and xml:space. These are copied into the element node except if a declaration of the same attribute is already in the attribute axis of the element (whether or not it is included in the document subset). This search and copying are omitted from the Exclusive XML Canonicalization method.

parameterdescription.para: the visibility of the parent node

simplesect.para: 0 on success or -1 on fail.

briefdescription.para: Checks that current element node has no relative namespaces defined.

simplesect.para: 0 if the node has no relative namespaces or -1 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Canonical XML v 1.0 (http://www.w3.org/TR/xml-c14n)

detaileddescription.para: Element Nodes If the element is not in the node-set, then the result is obtained by processing the namespace axis, then the attribute axis, then processing the child nodes of the element that are in the node-set (in document order). If the element is in the node-set, then the result is an open angle bracket (<), the element QName, the result of processing the namespace axis, the result of processing the attribute axis, a close angle bracket (>), the result of processing the child nodes of the element that are in the node-set (in document order), an open angle bracket, a forward slash (/), the element QName, and a close angle bracket.

parameterdescription.para: the pointer to C14N context object

simplesect.para: non-negative value on success or negative value on fail

briefdescription.para: Cleanups the C14N context object.

parameterdescription.para: the pointer to C14N context object

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDocPtr doc, xmlC14NIsVisibleCallback is_visible_callback, void *user_data, xmlC14NMode mode, xmlChar **inclusive_ns_prefixes, int with_comments, xmlOutputBufferPtr buf)
memberdef.name: xmlC14NNewCtx

briefdescription.para: Creates new C14N context object to store C14N parameters.

parameterdescription.para: the function to use to determine is node visible or not
the first parameter for is_visible_callback function (in most cases, it is nodes set)
the list of inclusive namespace prefixes ended with a NULL or NULL if there is no inclusive namespaces (only for ` canonicalization)
include comments in the result (!=0) or not (==0)
the output buffer to store canonical XML; this buffer MUST have encoder==NULL because C14N requires UTF-8 output

simplesect.para: pointer to newly created object (success) or NULL (fail)

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, xmlC14NIsVisibleCallback is_visible_callback, void *user_data, int mode, xmlChar **inclusive_ns_prefixes, int with_comments, xmlOutputBuffer *buf)
memberdef.name: xmlC14NExecute

briefdescription.para: Dumps the canonized image of given XML document into the provided buffer.

detaileddescription.para: For details see "Canonical XML" (http://www.w3.org/TR/xml-c14n) or "Exclusive XML Canonicalization" (http://www.w3.org/TR/xml-exc-c14n)

parameterdescription.para: the function to use to determine is node visible or not
the first parameter for is_visible_callback function (in most cases, it is nodes set)
the list of inclusive namespace prefixes ended with a NULL or NULL if there is no inclusive namespaces (only for exclusive canonicalization, ignored otherwise)
include comments in the result (!=0) or not (==0)
the output buffer to store canonical XML; this buffer MUST have encoder==NULL because C14N requires UTF-8 output

simplesect.para: non-negative value on success or a negative value on fail

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, xmlNodeSet *nodes, int mode, xmlChar **inclusive_ns_prefixes, int with_comments, xmlOutputBuffer *buf)
memberdef.name: xmlC14NDocSaveTo

briefdescription.para: Dumps the canonized image of given XML document into the provided buffer.

detaileddescription.para: For details see "Canonical XML" (http://www.w3.org/TR/xml-c14n) or "Exclusive XML Canonicalization" (http://www.w3.org/TR/xml-exc-c14n)

parameterdescription.para: the nodes set to be included in the canonized image or NULL if all document nodes should be included
the list of inclusive namespace prefixes ended with a NULL or NULL if there is no inclusive namespaces (only for exclusive canonicalization, ignored otherwise)
include comments in the result (!=0) or not (==0)
the output buffer to store canonical XML; this buffer MUST have encoder==NULL because C14N requires UTF-8 output

simplesect.para: non-negative value on success or a negative value on fail

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, xmlNodeSet *nodes, int mode, xmlChar **inclusive_ns_prefixes, int with_comments, xmlChar **doc_txt_ptr)
memberdef.name: xmlC14NDocDumpMemory

briefdescription.para: Dumps the canonized image of given XML document into memory.

detaileddescription.para: For details see "Canonical XML" (http://www.w3.org/TR/xml-c14n) or "Exclusive XML Canonicalization" (http://www.w3.org/TR/xml-exc-c14n)

parameterdescription.para: the nodes set to be included in the canonized image or NULL if all document nodes should be included
the list of inclusive namespace prefixes ended with a NULL or NULL if there is no inclusive namespaces (only for exclusive canonicalization, ignored otherwise)
include comments in the result (!=0) or not (==0)
the memory pointer for allocated canonical XML text; the caller of this functions is responsible for calling xmlFree() to free allocated memory

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written on success or a negative value on fail

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, xmlNodeSet *nodes, int mode, xmlChar **inclusive_ns_prefixes, int with_comments, const char *filename, int compression)
memberdef.name: xmlC14NDocSave

briefdescription.para: Dumps the canonized image of given XML document into the file.

detaileddescription.para: For details see "Canonical XML" (http://www.w3.org/TR/xml-c14n) or "Exclusive XML Canonicalization" (http://www.w3.org/TR/xml-exc-c14n)

parameterdescription.para: the nodes set to be included in the canonized image or NULL if all document nodes should be included
the list of inclusive namespace prefixes ended with a NULL or NULL if there is no inclusive namespaces (only for exclusive canonicalization, ignored otherwise)
include comments in the result (!=0) or not (==0)
the filename to store canonical XML image
the compression level (zlib required): -1 - libxml default, 0 - uncompressed, >0 - compression level

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written success or a negative value on fail
